title: Classify triangles by side lengths
description: Practice identifying triangles by their side-lengths as equilateral, isosceles, or scalene.
topic_path: Module 4: Angle measure and plane figures
Topic D: Two-dimensional figures and symmetry

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{XYZ}$ have?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle{XYZ}$ has $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$, so it also has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{XYZ}$ is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle and an $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle XYZ. Side XY is 5 units long, side XZ is 5 units long, and side YZ is 5 units long. The angles at X, Y, and Z are acute angles.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{DEF}$ have?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle{DEF}$ has $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$, so it also has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{DEF}$ is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle and an $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle DEF. Side DE is 7 units long, side DF is 7 units long, and side EF is 7 units long. The angles at D, E, and F are acute angles.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{ABC}$ have?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle{ABC}$ has $\purpleC{2\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{ABC}$ is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle ABC. The angle at A has a box at its vertex connecting lines AB and AC. Side AB is 5 units long, side AC is 5 units long, and side BC is 7 units long. The angles at B and C are acute angles.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{MNO}$ have?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle{MNO}$ has $\purpleC{2\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{MNO}$ is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle MNO. Side MN is 5 units long, side MO is 5 units long, and side NO is 4 units long. The angles at M, N, and O are acute angles.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{DEF}$ have?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\triangle{DEF}$ has $\purpleC{2\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{DEF}$ is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle DEF. Side DF is 4 units long, side FE is 4 units long, and side DE is 7 units long. The angles at D and E are acute angles. The angle at F is obtuse angle.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{STU}$ have?

$\triangle{STU}$ has $\greenD{0\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{STU}$ is a $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle STU. Side ST is 9 units long, side SU is 6 units long, and side UT is 7 units long. The angles at S, T, and U are acute angles.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{JKL}$ have?

$\triangle{JKL}$ has $\greenD{0\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{JKL}$ is a $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle JKL. Side JK is 4 units long, side KL is 5 units long, and side JL is 7 units long. The angles at J and L are acute angles. The angle at K is obtuse angle.

hints:
###What are the types of triangles?

An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$. 

An  $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A  $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

###How many sides of equal length does $\triangle{PQR}$ have?

$\triangle{PQR}$ has $\greenD{0\text{ equal sides}}$.

###The answer

$\triangle{PQR}$ is a $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle.

options.alt: A triangle PQR. The angle at R has a box at its vertex connecting lines RP and RQ. Side RP is 5 units long, side RQ is 12 units long, and side PQ is 13 units long. The angles at P and Q are acute angles.

**Complete the table below to classify the triangles as scalene or isosceles.**

Triangle | Classification
- | - | -
![A triangle with side lengths of 7, 8, and 10 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d524c24c71b2cfc61d52773fe268d7864599d8f2) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 3, and 5 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ebdb24946da8907b50abbf3eee87680655da9bc0) |  [[☃ dropdown 2]]

hints:
An $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$  triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 or 3 equal sides}}$.

A $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 equal sides}}$.

This triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 sides of equal length}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is a $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle.

This triangle has $\purpleC{\text{2 sides of equal length}}$.  

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is an $\purpleC{\text{isosceles}}$ triangle.

This triangle has $\greenD{\text{0 sides of equal length}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is a $\greenD{\text{scalene}}$ triangle.

Here is the completed table:

Triangle | Classification
- | - | -
![A triangle with side lengths of 7, 8, and 10 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d524c24c71b2cfc61d52773fe268d7864599d8f2) | Scalene
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 3, and 5 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ebdb24946da8907b50abbf3eee87680655da9bc0) |  Isosceles
![A triangle with side lengths of 4, 5, and 2 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/37cea2a052c5a7856c673861c9b8f355f7a55a3d) |  Scalene

assessment_items.question_content:
**Complete the table below to classify the triangles as equilateral or not equilateral.**

Triangle | Classification
- | - | -
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 4, and 6 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/863bc1bcfcbc9e65b3d0a897ab815362ad91eca4) | [[☃ dropdown 1]]
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 3, and 3 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b00f35a4854eb2277afd427c2643878a68ccc9b8)|  [[☃ dropdown 4]]
![A triangle with side lengths of 1, 10, and 10 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d78949d0720d4df59fba21ee55a129dd87e1ed6f) |   [[☃ dropdown 5]]
hints: An $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle has  $\blueD{\text{3 equal sides}}$.

This triangle has $0$ sides of equal length.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is **not** an equilateral triangle.

This triangle has $\blueD{\text{3 sides of equal length}}$.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is an $\blueD{\text{equilateral}}$ triangle.

This triangle has $2$ sides of equal length.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, it is **not** an equilateral triangle.

Here is the completed table:

Triangle | Classification
- | - | -
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 4, and 6 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/863bc1bcfcbc9e65b3d0a897ab815362ad91eca4) | Not equilateral
![A triangle with side lengths of 3, 3, and 3 units.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b00f35a4854eb2277afd427c2643878a68ccc9b8)|  Equilateral
![A triangle with side lengths of 1, 10, and 10 units. ](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d78949d0720d4df59fba21ee55a129dd87e1ed6f) |   Not equilateral
